apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Ioc
  <;> (try norm_num) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Prove that Φ₅ r is continuous on [0, 1]
      -- This is already given by Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      exact Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Prove that Φ₅ r is continuous on (0, 1]
      -- This is already given by Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      exact Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Prove that Φ₅ r is bounded on (0, 1]
      -- This follows from the fact that Φ₅ r is continuous on [0, 1] and [0, 1] is compact
      -- and hence Φ₅ r is bounded on [0, 1], and therefore also on (0, 1]
      exact by
        have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) := by
          exact Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn.mono (Set.Ioc_subset_Icc_self)
        have h₂ : BddAbove (Φ₅ r '' Set.Icc 0 1) := by
          apply IsCompact.bddAbove_image
          · exact isCompact_Icc
          · exact h₁
        have h₃ : BddAbove (Φ₅ r '' Set.Ioc 0 1) := by
          have h₄ : Φ₅ r '' Set.Ioc 0 1 ⊆ Φ₅ r '' Set.Icc 0 1 := by
            intro y hy
            rcases hy with ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
            have h₅ : x ∈ Set.Icc 0 1 := by
              constructor <;> (try norm_num at hx ⊢) <;> (try linarith [hx.1, hx.2])
              <;> (try simp_all [Set.Ioc, Set.Icc]) <;> (try linarith)
            exact ⟨x, h₅, rfl⟩
          exact BddAbove.mono h₄ h₂
        exact h₃
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Prove that Φ₅ r is bounded on [0, 1]
      -- This follows from the fact that Φ₅ r is continuous on [0, 1] and [0, 1] is compact
      exact by
        have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) := Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
        have h₂ : BddAbove (Φ₅ r '' Set.Icc 0 1) := by
          apply IsCompact.bddAbove_image
          · exact isCompact_Icc
          · exact h₁
        exact h₂
    })

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₅ r x) (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₅ r x) (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : ∀ x ∈ Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1, (fun x : ℝ => Φ₅ r x) x = Φ₅ r x := by simp
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on a bounded interval to conclude it is integrable
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₅ r x) (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) :=
      (hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuousOn (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) (by continuity) (by
        -- Verify that the interval has finite measure
        have h₅ : MeasureTheory.Measure (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) < ∞ := by
          have h₅₁ : MeasureTheory.Measure (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) = (1 : ℝ) := by
            simp [volume_Ioc]
          rw [h₅₁]
          norm_num
          <;> linarith
        exact h₅
      )).congr' (by simp)
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₅ r) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn.mono (by
      intro x hx
      simp only [Ioc, Set.mem_Ioc, Icc, Set.mem_Icc] at hx ⊢
      <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try {
        constructor <;>
        (try { linarith }) <;>
        (try {
          cases' hx with hx₁ hx₂ <;>
          linarith
        })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        cases' hx with hx₁ hx₂ <;>
        linarith
      })
    )
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₄ : ∃ (C : ℝ), ∀ x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₅ r is continuous on [0,1] to find a bound
      have h₅ : ContinuousOn (fun x => |(Φ₅ r) x|) (Icc 0 1) :=
        ContinuousOn.abs h₂
      have h₆ : ∃ C, ∀ x ∈ Icc 0 1, |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := by
        -- Use the extreme value theorem to find the maximum of |Φ₅ r|
        have h₇ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
        have h₈ : ContinuousOn (fun x => |(Φ₅ r) x|) (Icc 0 1) := h₅
        have h₉ : ∃ C, ∀ x ∈ Icc 0 1, |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := by
          -- Use the extreme value theorem
          have h₁₀ : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc 0 1 ∧ ∀ y ∈ Icc 0 1, |(Φ₅ r) y| ≤ |(Φ₅ r) x| := by
            -- Use the extreme value theorem for continuous functions on compact sets
            have h₁₁ : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc 0 1 ∧ ∀ y ∈ Icc 0 1, |(Φ₅ r) y| ≤ |(Φ₅ r) x| := by
              -- Use the extreme value theorem for continuous functions on compact sets
              have h₁₂ : ∃ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc 0 1 ∧ ∀ y ∈ Icc 0 1, |(Φ₅ r) y| ≤ |(Φ₅ r) x| := by
                apply IsCompact.exists_forall_ge h₃ (Set.nonempty_Icc.mpr (by norm_num))
                exact h₅
              exact h₁₂
            exact h₁₁
          obtain ⟨x, hx, hx'⟩ := h₁₀
          refine' ⟨|(Φ₅ r) x|, _⟩
          intro y hy
          exact hx' y hy
        exact h₉
      exact h₆
    obtain ⟨C, hC⟩ := h₄
    have h₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₅ r) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₅ r is bounded and continuous to show that it has finite integral
      have h₆ : (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = 1 := by
        -- Compute the measure of (0,1]
        simp [volume_Ioc]
        <;> norm_num
      have h₇ : ∃ (C : ℝ), ∀ x ∈ (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ), |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := by
        -- Use the bound on [0,1] to get a bound on (0,1]
        refine' ⟨C, _⟩
        intro x hx
        have h₈ : x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
          simp only [Ioc, Set.mem_Ioc, Icc, Set.mem_Icc] at hx ⊢
          constructor <;> (try { linarith }) <;> (try { linarith })
        exact hC x h₈
      -- Use the boundedness to show finite integral
      have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₅ r) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
        have h₉ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (C : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
          -- The constant function C is integrable
          have h₁₀ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (C : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
            -- Use the fact that the measure of (0,1] is finite
            have h₁₁ : (volume.restrict (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1)) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = 1 := by
              simp [volume_Ioc]
              <;> norm_num
            -- Use the fact that the constant function is integrable on a set of finite measure
            have h₁₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (C : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
              have h₁₃ : ∫ (x : ℝ) in (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ), (C : ℝ) ∂volume := C * (1 : ℝ) := by
                simp [volume_Ioc]
                <;> norm_num
                <;> ring_nf
                <;> field_simp
                <;> norm_num
              have h₁₄ : ∫ (x : ℝ) in (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ), (C : ℝ) ∂volume < ∞ := by
                simp [h₁₃]
                <;>
                  (try { linarith }) <;>
                  (try { norm_num }) <;>
                  (try { exact by positivity }) <;>
                  (try { exact by linarith })
              have h₁₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (C : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
                refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_norm_bounded _ (fun x hx => by
                  simp_all [Real.norm_eq_abs, abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos, le_of_lt]) h₁₄
                <;>
                  (try { linarith }) <;>
                  (try { exact by positivity })
              exact h₁₅
            exact h₁₂
          exact h₁₀
        -- Use the boundedness to show finite integral
        have h₁₀ : |(Φ₅ r)| ≤ fun x => (C : ℝ) := by
          intro x
          have h₁₁ : x ∈ Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := by
            intro hx
            have h₁₂ : x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
              simp only [Ioc, Set.mem_Ioc, Icc, Set.mem_Icc] at hx ⊢
              constructor <;> (try { linarith }) <;> (try { linarith })
            exact hC x h₁₂
          by_cases hx : x ∈ Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1
          · have h₁₃ : |(Φ₅ r) x| ≤ C := h₁₁ hx
            simp_all [abs_le]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> linarith
          · simp_all [Set.mem_Ioc, Set.mem_Icc]
            <;>
            (try {
              norm_num at *
              <;>
              linarith
            })
            <;>
            (try {
              simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos, le_of_lt]
              <;>
              norm_num
              <;>
              linarith
            })
        -- Use the comparison test
        have h₁₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x => (C : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := h₉
        have h₁₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₅ r) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
          -- Use the comparison test
          refine' (hasFiniteIntegral_of_norm_bounded (by
            -- Show that the norm of Φ₅ r is bounded by the constant function C
            have h₁₃ : |(Φ₅ r)| ≤ fun x => (C : ℝ) := h₁₀
            refine' (h₁₃).trans _
            -- Use the fact that the constant function C is integrable
            simp [Pi.le_def]
            <;>
              (try { linarith }) <;>
              (try { norm_num }) <;>
              (try { exact by positivity }) <;>
              (try { exact by linarith })
          )) _ (by simpa using h₁₁)
          <;>
            (try { linarith }) <;>
            (try { norm_num }) <;>
            (try { exact by positivity }) <;>
            (try { exact by linarith })
        exact h₁₂
      exact h₈
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

-- Prove that the integral of Φ₅ r over (0,1] is finite
  have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₅ r) (volume.restrict (Ioc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₅ r is continuous and the interval is compact
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : MeasurableSet (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ) := measurableSet_Ioc
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous and the interval is bounded
    refine' ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral _ (by positivity) (by
      -- Show that the function is bounded on (0,1]
      refine' ContinuousOn.bounded _ _
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try exact h₂) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the interval is compact
          exact isCompact_Icc
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the interval is compact
          exact isCompact_Icc
        })
      )
    -- Show that the function is continuous on (0,1]
    exact h₂
  -- Use the fact that the function has finite integral to conclude the proof
  simpa [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integrable] using h₁

    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
      have h₃ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
        have h₄ : ContinuousOn (Φ₅ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
          apply ContinuousOn.mono (Φ₅_contDiffOn.continuousOn)

            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try linarith) <;>
            (try
              {
                intro x hx
                simp at hx ⊢
                <;> linarith

        exact h₄
